subject: Human Resources
title: Engagement vs. Satisfaction
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Engagement vs. Satisfaction.

hook.text: A satisfied employee shows up on time. An engaged employee shows up with ideas. There's a massive difference.

buildup.text: Satisfaction means employees don't hate their job. Engagement means they're emotionally invested in it.

discovery.text: Gallup found only 23% of employees worldwide are engaged—the rest are satisfied at best, or actively disengaged.

twist.text: A satisfied employee won't leave but won't go the extra mile either. They stay comfortable, not committed.

climax.text: Engagement drives discretionary effort—doing more than required because people genuinely care about the outcome.

punchline.text: Satisfaction keeps people. Engagement moves them.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between satisfaction and engagement?
options: Satisfaction is contentment; engagement is emotional investment
Satisfaction is always better than engagement
